page: Microsoft Azure E32-16s_v6 is an x86_64 architecture server belonging to the Esv6 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features a dedicated Intel Xeon 8573C processor operating at 4.2 GHz, configured with 8 physical cores and 16 vCPUs via hyperthreading. The memory allocation is 256.0 GB, yielding a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 16.0 GB per vCPU. Cache specifications include 48 KB L1d, 2 MB L2, and 260 MB L3 cache. This instance does not include local storage or GPUs, but it is Premium Storage capable.

Benchmark data shows strong single-core performance, with a Geekbench single-core score of 2000.0 and a single-threaded gzip compression rate of 31.0 GB/sec, both placing in the top 25%. Text processing also performs in the top 25% at 2644.0 on Geekbench. Multi-core and multi-threaded operations generally fall into the middle 50% tier, including a stress-ng multi-core score of 15985.02 and a Geekbench multi-core score of 12143.0. For LLM workloads, the server achieves top-tier performance in text generation for small 135M models at 373.33 tokens/sec, while prompt processing and larger model inference remain in the average tier.

A notable tradeoff is the memory subsystem performance; the Passmark memory score of 1783.15 falls into the bottom 25%, despite the large 256.0 GB capacity. Large-block memory bandwidth is measured at 83.49 GB/sec for read and 49.69 GB/sec for write operations. From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the high memory density per vCPU makes this server appropriate for memory-bound workloads that do not require high-speed local storage or GPU acceleration, allowing users to avoid paying for unneeded hardware components.

Given the high memory capacity and strong single-core performance, this server is configured for memory-intensive applications such as database operations, caching layers like Redis, which achieved 22.54 million SET operations per second in testing, and memory-bound enterprise applications. It is also suitable for lightweight LLM inference and text processing tasks.
description: The Microsoft Azure E32-16s_v6 is an x86_64 memory-optimized server from the Esv6 family, powered by a dedicated Intel Xeon 8573C processor running at 4.2 GHz. It features 16 vCPUs across 8 physical cores, paired with 256.0 GB of RAM, providing a high ratio of 16.0 GB of memory per vCPU. The server lacks local storage and GPUs but supports Premium Storage. Performance benchmarks indicate strong single-core capabilities, with Geekbench single-core and single-threaded gzip compression scores in the top 25%. However, the Passmark memory score falls in the bottom 25%, highlighting a tradeoff in memory subsystem performance. This server is configured for memory-intensive workloads, database operations, and caching systems like Redis, where high memory capacity is required without the need for local NVMe drives or hardware accelerators.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E32-16s_v6 is an Esv6-family memory-optimized server featuring 16 vCPUs, 256.0 GB of RAM, and an Intel Xeon 8573C processor, designed for memory-intensive database and caching workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure E32-16s_v6 is an Esv6 memory-optimized server with 16 vCPUs, 256 GB RAM, and an Intel Xeon CPU, built for database workloads.
tagline: An x86_64 memory-optimized server featuring 256 GB of RAM and dedicated Intel Xeon processing for memory-intensive database and caching workloads.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon 8573C processor with 16 dedicated vCPUs and 4.2 GHz clock speed
256.0 GB of RAM providing a high 16.0 GB per vCPU memory ratio
Strong single-core performance in Geekbench and gzip compression benchmarks
No local storage or GPU resources, optimized for external Premium Storage
Fits memory-bound applications, Redis caching, and database operations
